seo.title: Building Workflow Automation with Google ADK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build workflow automation with Google ADK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building Workflow Automation with Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK) enables sophisticated multi-agent systems powered by Gemini models for multi-step task execution, API integrations, and human-in-the-loop approvals. Guardrails are essential to prevent circular dependencies in agent delegation, output inconsistencies from model switching, session state corruption, and tool response mismatches. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure action correctness, permission validation, idempotency, and rollback capability in medium-risk workflows.

architectureOverview.heading: Workflow Automation Architecture with Google ADK
architectureOverview.description:
The typical Google ADK workflow automation architecture features a Supervisor Agent that orchestrates specialized Worker Agents for tasks like API calls, data processing, and approval routing. Each agent leverages Gemini models with session state management for long-running conversations, incorporating conditional logic for branching execution paths and human-in-the-loop gates for critical decisions.

API integrations occur through tool-equipped agents that handle external service calls, while delegation patterns enable task handoff between agents. Session persistence maintains context across steps, but introduces risks like state corruption. The architecture supports idempotent operations and rollback mechanisms via structured state tracking.

Verification focuses on ensuring actions match intended outcomes, permissions are validated before API calls, operations are idempotent to handle retries safely, and rollback paths exist for error recovery.
architectureOverview.diagram:
Supervisor Agent
    |
    +--> API Worker Agent --> External APIs
    |
    +--> Approval Worker --> Human-in-Loop
    |
    +--> Data Processor --> Conditional Logic
    |
Vex Guardrails (before_agent_callback)
    ↑
Google ADK Session State

risks.name: Multi-agent delegation creating circular dependencies
risks.description: Supervisor endlessly delegates between agents without termination conditions
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies delegation graphs and enforces max delegation depth

risks.name: Gemini model switching causing output inconsistency
risks.description: Different Gemini variants produce incompatible tool response formats
risks.mitigation: Vex standardizes output schemas across model variants

risks.name: Session state corruption in long conversations
risks.description: State mutations during parallel agent execution corrupt workflow context
risks.mitigation: Vex snapshots and validates session state before agent execution

risks.name: Tool response format mismatches across agents
risks.description: Agents expect different JSON schemas from shared API tools
risks.mitigation: Vex enforces consistent tool response contracts

implementation.title: Basic Supervisor Agent Setup
implementation.description: Create a supervisor agent that delegates to worker agents based on task type
implementation.code:
from google_adk.agents import SupervisorAgent, WorkerAgent
from google_adk.session import Session

supervisor = SupervisorAgent(
    model='gemini-2.0-flash-exp',
    tools=[],
    instructions="Delegate tasks to appropriate worker agents"
)

api_worker = WorkerAgent(
    model='gemini-2.0-flash-exp',
    tools=[api_tool],
    instructions="Handle API integrations"
)

session = Session(supervisor)
session.run('Process customer order with payment approval')
